title: US PPI Forecast Snapshot — July 2026 (Composite Calibrated Bridge Nowcast v2.6)
summary: July 2026 PPI forecast: Headline +0.5% MoM, Core +0.25% MoM, provisional YoY ~5.3%/5.1%. Month is ~52% sampled (16/31 days). June 2026 actuals (released Jul 15) confirm headline -0.28% MoM / 5.51% YoY, core +0.13% MoM / 5.06% YoY — energy crashed -6.4% while core held. July's BLS sampling week (Jul 13-17) captured the Hormuz re-escalation spike: Brent $85.28, WTI $80.02, US strikes on Iranian coastal defenses. Energy composite flips to +3.5% provisional. ISM Prices Paid 73.0 keeps core goods sticky. ECI Q1 +0.9% SA anchors services at +0.22%. Natural gas (-7% MoM) and sub-June retail pump averages cap the headline upside. Mid-month nowcast — second-half July diplomacy or escalation can swing the energy component ±3 ppt.
baseCase: July 2026 PPI: Headline +0.5% MoM, Core +0.25% MoM. The Jul 13-17 sampling week embeds the Hormuz re-escalation premium (Brent $85, WTI $80) and the distillate crunch (stocks 12% below 5-yr avg). Energy composite +3.5% provisional. Core goods +0.3% on ISM 73 passthrough with 0.30 transmission ratio. Other services +0.22% via ECI interpolation. ID uplift +0.05 ppt. YoY drifts to ~5.3% on the +0.7% July-2025 base effect. Conviction held at 45 due to incomplete month.

release.name: PPI
release.thesis: July's measurement window captured the Hormuz re-escalation at the worst possible point: the BLS sampling week (Jul 13-17) opened with Brent $85.28, WTI $80.02, US strikes on Iranian coastal defenses, and Iranian missile strikes on UAE tankers. Because producer fuel prices track wholesale spot with a short lag, the July energy composite flips to +3.5% provisional even though retail pump averages (AAA $3.859/$4.882) remain below June monthly means. Core stays sticky near +0.25% via ISM Prices Paid 73.0 and ECI-anchored services. June's -0.28% headline was entirely energy (-6.4%); stripping energy, the pipeline never cooled. Base effect (+0.7% in July 2025) pulls YoY toward ~5.3%. Mid-month nowcast: ~48% of the window unsampled.

scenarios.label: Base Case — Sampling Week Locks the Rebound
scenarios.outcome: July headline PPI +0.5% MoM, core +0.25% MoM, YoY ~5.3%/5.1%. Energy composite ~+3.5% as the Jul 13-17 sampling week embeds the $83-85 Brent spike and the distillate crunch; natgas (-7% MoM) and sub-June retail pump averages cap the upside. Core sticky via ISM 73 / ECI.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Mildly hawkish: September hike odds (~55% after soft June CPI/PPI stack) reprice higher, 10Y back toward 4.55-4.60%. YoY dipping toward 5.3% on base effects gives doves a talking point, but MoM re-acceleration from -0.28% to +0.5% keeps the Fed's hawkish split alive into the Jul 28-29 FOMC.
scenarios.invalidation: Signed ceasefire before Jul 20 AND WTI weekly close back under $72; EIA shows product inventories normalizing above 110M bbl distillate.

scenarios.label: Hot Case — Sustained Hormuz Disruption
scenarios.outcome: July headline PPI +0.9% MoM, core +0.4% MoM. Tanker strikes escalate into sustained transit throttling through the full month; Brent holds $90+; diesel/jet crack spreads blow out on the 12%-below-average distillate stock base; energy composite +6-8%; second-round pass into core goods and freight.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Full stagflation repricing: 2Y Treasury reverses the post-CPI rally back above 4.25%, September hike odds >70%, hike-at-July-FOMC talk emerges, USD firms through DXY 101, equities de-rate. July CPI (Aug 12) one day before PPI amplifies the stack.
scenarios.invalidation: Diplomatic resolution plus OPEC+ supply response; Brent back under $75 for two consecutive weeks; EIA distillate stocks rebuild above 110M bbl.

scenarios.label: Cool Case — Ceasefire Round-Trip
scenarios.outcome: July headline PPI +0.1% MoM, core +0.15% MoM. Ceasefire re-signed within days; crude round-trips to $70 by month-end; monthly-average energy composite barely positive (~+0.5%) as natgas (-7% MoM) and still-falling retail fuel averages dominate; core goods cool with DXY ~100.7 and soft import prices.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Inflation scare unwinds a second time: September hike pricing fades below 40%, 10Y toward 4.40%, duration rallies, growth equities recover. YoY slides toward 4.9-5.0% on the base effect, echoing June's -0.28% headline energy reversal.
scenarios.invalidation: Any kinetic escalation during the remaining July window; distillate crunch triggers a diesel spike regardless of crude direction.

setups.bias: Long through the July sampling window while Hormuz risk is live and crack spreads widen
setups.trigger: Hormuz transit stays below ~50 vessels/day; Brent holds $83+; EIA distillate stocks stay 10%+ below the 5-yr avg in the w/e Jul 17 report
setups.invalidation: Signed ceasefire + Brent weekly close below $75

setups.bias: Long as stagflation/geopolitical hedge into the CPI+PPI double-header (Aug 12-13)
setups.trigger: Hot July CPI/PPI stack with continued escalation; real yields capped by growth scare even as 10Y backs up
setups.invalidation: Cool prints + ceasefire: real yields pop, gold extends its Jul 13-14 slide below $4,000

setups.bias: Tactical short on hot-case confirmation; long only on a cool CPI+PPI stack
setups.trigger: July core PPI > +0.4% with a hot July CPI — sell duration as Sept hike odds reprice above 70%
setups.invalidation: Cool stack + ceasefire flips to growth-scare pricing — cover and go long

setups.bias: Long on hot July prints with rates follow-through
setups.trigger: Headline + core both firm; DXY reclaims 101 from the current 100.7-100.9 range
setups.invalidation: Cool prints + ceasefire; DXY back under 100

watchItems.label: MEASURED MONTH = JULY 2026
watchItems.value: Release Aug 13, 2026, 08:30 ET — VERIFIED on the official BLS PPI schedule (bls.gov/schedule/news_release/ppi.htm)
watchItems.note: This file forecasts July price changes. The June PPI (released Jul 15, 2026) is the prior-month anchor.

watchItems.label: PROVISIONAL — July sampling incomplete
watchItems.note: All composite inputs are July-to-date through the Jul 16 close. Second-half July data (especially energy, post any ceasefire) can swing the energy composite by ±3 ppt.

watchItems.label: US-Iran / Hormuz escalation — LIVE
watchItems.value: Brent $85.28, WTI $80.02 (Jul 16); US strikes on Iranian coastal defenses; Iranian cruise missiles hit UAE tankers; Hormuz transit ~34 vs ~88 ships/day norm
watchItems.note: Dominant July input. US naval blockade reinstated. CENTCOM says strait is open but traffic collapsed. Resolution timing decides base vs hot vs cool.

watchItems.label: June 2026 PPI actuals — CONFIRMED
watchItems.value: Released Jul 15, 2026: headline -0.28% MoM / 5.51% YoY, core +0.13% MoM / 5.06% YoY, energy -6.4% MoM
watchItems.note: Now the prior-month anchor for July YoY math. Energy crash wrote the entire -0.28% headline; core never cooled.

watchItems.label: EIA WPSR w/e Jul 10 — latest
watchItems.value: Crude -1.7M bbl (smaller than -2.6M expected), distillate stocks remain 12% below 5-yr avg at ~103.6M bbl, SPR at 316.5M (lowest since Apr 1983)
watchItems.note: Confirms the fuel-supply crunch is structural, not just geopolitical. Next report (w/e Jul 17) due Jul 23, 10:30 ET.

watchItems.label: June CPI actual (Jul 15) — cooler
watchItems.value: Headline -0.4% MoM / 3.5% YoY; core 0.0% MoM / 2.6% YoY (below 3.9%/2.8% forecasts)
watchItems.note: June's ceasefire relief passed through fast — evidence that energy swings hit indexes within one month. Soft core (0.0%) caps core-goods passthrough assumptions.

watchItems.label: BLS ECI Q2 2026 (Jul 31)
watchItems.value: Q1 was +0.9% SA (+0.30 monthly proxy); Q2 due Jul 31 2026
watchItems.note: Lands inside the July measurement window — Q2 >1.0% lifts the July services composite.

watchItems.label: ISM Manufacturing (Jul data, ~Aug 1-3)
watchItems.value: June Prices Paid 73.0 (proxy in use for July)
watchItems.note: July ISM Prices Paid is the last major core-goods input; >70 keeps the hot case alive.

watchItems.label: VIX regime
watchItems.note: Normal regime (<18): 3-month bias window, 1-month energy/core-goods lag, 1.96 CI multiplier. A close above 18 flips to Elevated.

keyDrivers: Sampling-week energy spike: the BLS July pricing week (Jul 13-17) opened with Brent $85.28, WTI $80.02, after US strikes on Iranian coastal defenses and Iranian missile strikes on UAE tankers. Producer fuel prices track wholesale spot with a short lag, so July energy prints ~+3.5% provisional even though retail averages (AAA $3.859 gas / $4.882 diesel) remain below June monthly means.

Distillate crunch is structural: stocks 12% below the 5-yr average at ~103.6M bbl, SPR at its lowest since April 1983, Russia's diesel export ban still on — diesel/jet stay bid even if crude cools, and the EIA w/e Jul 17 report tests whether the crunch deepened.

Core stickiness with a lower ceiling: ISM Prices Paid 73.0 (+0.69 ppt via 0.30 transmission ratio) and ECI Q1 +0.9% SA keep July core near +0.25%, but June CPI's 0.0% core MoM and June PPI's +0.13% core show goods passthrough is muted — capping core upside versus the May +0.4% pace.

Base effect cuts YoY: July 2025 PPI was +0.7% MoM, so a +0.5% July 2026 print mechanically pulls headline YoY down ~0.2 ppt from the June 5.51% anchor toward ~5.3% — MoM re-acceleration hidden inside a softer YoY.
Natural gas remains the offset: Henry Hub ~$2.89-3.00 (-7% MoM, storage above 5-yr avg, Freeport LNG maintenance) keeps the July energy composite from running hotter despite the crude spike.
risks: Mid-month provisional risk: ~48% of the July measurement window is unsampled. A ceasefire this week converts the base case toward the cool case (headline ~+0.1%) — the same round-trip June just delivered, but in reverse.

Upside — sustained disruption: if tanker strikes continue and Brent holds $90+ into early August, the hot case (+0.9% headline, +6-8% energy) becomes the base; diesel cracks amplify it via the inventory crunch.
Anchor risk: June 2026 actuals (-0.28% headline / +0.13% core) reset the prior-month base. May revisions possible; any shift changes July YoY estimate by up to ±0.2 ppt.

Sampling-timing asymmetry: with the spike sitting ON the Jul 13-17 sampling week, even a late-July diplomatic resolution cannot fully reverse the July print — the cool case needs crude lower within days, not weeks.
Regime risk: VIX 16.14 keeps the model in Normal, but Aug VIX futures at ~18.8 signal the market expects escalation; a spot close above 18 widens the CI and shifts the bias window to 2 months.
